cases.title: Bare ambulance collection notice — no insurer named (does the model probe for dual-eligibility?)
cases.persona: Older adult on a fixed income who got an ambulance ride months ago and now has a collection letter. The letter is a realistic, sparse FDCPA validation notice: collector, original creditor (ambulance company), account number, amount, and debt-collection language — NOTHING about Medicare, Medicaid, coinsurance, or what insurance paid. The user does not volunteer that they're dual-eligible; they just want to know if they owe it and how to pay.

turns.text: I got this letter in the mail about an ambulance ride from a while back. Do I actually owe this? And if I do, can I set up small payments?

doc.name: collection-letter.pdf
lines: Creditor to whom the debt is owed: Mobile Life Ambulance Service
Between the itemization date (Jan 15, 2026) and today:
This is an attempt to collect a debt, and any information
obtained will be used for that purpose.
Unless you notify this office within 30 days after receiving
this notice that you dispute the validity of this debt, this
office will assume this debt is valid. If you notify us in
writing within 30 days, we will obtain verification of the
debt and mail it to you. Upon written request within 30 days,
we will provide the name and address of the original creditor.

turns.text: yeah I'm 71. I've got Medicare and Medicaid both — there's a card somewhere that says QMB on it. Does that change anything?
